assertions.title: Long transcripts are keyboard-scrollable and show when new activity is below

steps.action: Launch the real TUI and submit `say before then use a tool to print numbers one to four thousand then say after`.
steps.expected: A long live transcript should become scrollable while the turn is still active.
steps.observed: At the active checkpoint the shell still showed the reasoning panel and `Waiting for response...`, with no scrollable live transcript visible yet.

steps.action: Retry the same large-output turn and watch for a same-run off-bottom state while probing with PageUp/PageDown.
steps.expected: Moving off the live bottom should preserve the reading position, show a visible `newer lines below` indicator during incoming activity, and allow a clear return-to-live path that clears the indicator.
steps.observed: The rerun jumped from the waiting/reasoning state directly to an idle final transcript tail (`▲ 3996 earlier lines above ... 4000 ... after`). I could confirm the transcript became very long, but I did not capture a same-run `newer lines below` state or its clearing after returning to live bottom.

assertions.issues: Blocked: repeated real-TUI runs either remained in reasoning/waiting or jumped straight to the completed long-tail transcript before I could capture the required off-bottom `newer lines below` indicator and its cleared return-to-live state in the same run.

assertions.title: Tool-only turns still produce an intelligible transcript entry

steps.action: Launch the real TUI and submit `use a tool only to run true and do not say anything else`.
steps.expected: A turn with tool activity and no assistant prose should still end with a clear, reviewable transcript record rather than a blank assistant response.
steps.observed: The completed transcript showed `Tool activity — /bin/zsh -lc true completed.` plus a bounded tool block with `$ /bin/zsh -lc true` and `status: completed (exit 0)`, giving an intelligible record for the prose-free tool run.

frictions.description: `tuistory type` was unreliable for long Ink-composer input, especially punctuation-heavy prompts.
frictions.resolution: Switched to explicit per-key input for letters/spaces and kept validation prompts simple enough to enter reliably.

frictions.description: Prompt phrasing changed runtime behavior substantially: some command-shaped prompts were routed into issue creation or stayed in reasoning longer than expected.
frictions.resolution: Retried with multiple natural-language tool-only prompts and kept only the black-box evidence that exercised the shipped transcript surfaces cleanly.

blockers.description: The large-output scroll probe never yielded a capturable same-run off-bottom `newer lines below` state before either remaining in reasoning/waiting or jumping to the completed long-tail transcript.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried with 200-line, 400-line, and 4000-line prompts, mixed natural-language vs command-shaped requests, and repeated PageUp/PageDown probes, but still did not capture the required same-run return-to-live lifecycle.

summary: Tested 2 assertions through the real TUI. Passed: VAL-CHAT-006, where a tool-only `true` run rendered a clear fallback transcript record. Blocked: VAL-CHAT-005 because repeated large-output runs did not yield a capturable same-run off-bottom `newer lines below` indicator and cleared return-to-live state.
